steps.title: Compare $f(x+1)$ to $f(x)$
steps.content: Compute $f(x+1)$ and compare it to $f(x)$: $$f(x+1) = 66b^{x+1} = 66 \cdot b^x \cdot b = b \cdot f(x)$$ So increasing $x$ by $1$ multiplies the output by $b$.

steps.title: Convert the growth factor to a percent increase
steps.content: The percentage increase from $f(x)$ to $b \cdot f(x)$ is: $$\frac{b \cdot f(x) - f(x)}{f(x)} \times 100 = (b - 1) \times 100$$ Therefore $c = 100(b - 1)$.

steps.title: Apply the c constraint
steps.content: Since $0 \lt c \lt 100$, we need $0 \lt 100(b-1) \lt 100$, which means $1 \lt b \lt 2$. This confirms $b$ is a growth factor slightly above $1$, consistent with the constraint.
